metadata.title:
W. W. P. FRAMING UP


POLITICAL FUTURE

pages.raw_text:
According to recent statement
the w. W. P. Co. is now willing to
recognize the union by meeting
with committees and will pay the
union scale to its linemen and
electricians. 1t is not stated that
the company will agree to the
"closed shop" as has been done
by the Traction Co.


There is a we'll founded belief
that the change of front by the big
power monopoly is due to the de-
feat of its candidate for mayor in
the recent city election. Whisper
ing Harry Blocker now realizes
that te old order of things has
passed away and tht he will have
to trot out his candidates for
county Offices and have them con-
firmed by the people in direct
primary election If the company is
to maintain its hold on the court
house.


Under these circumstances it is
not strange that the w. w. P. Co.
feels the necessity of making an
effort to conciliate not only labor
but the public generally.


Had either Daggett or Doust
been successful in the recent city
election there is no reason to be-
lievc that Whispering Harry and
General Manager Huntington
would be willing now to meet a
union committee. II they could
have continued in control of the
city in spite of the dinner pail
brigade" they would have taken
pleasure in continuing their policy
Of antagonism to organized labor


With a county election approach
ing the company realizes that it
